title: astragalus sanguinolentus var. illyricus
intro: Astragalus sanguinolentus var. illyricus, also known as Illyrian milkvetch or Illyrian astragalus, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including enhancing immune function, reducing stress, and improving cardiovascular health, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Astragalus sanguinolentus var. illyricus is sometimes used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and capsules, but its safety and efficacy should be evaluated by a healthcare professional before consumption.
